formats.format: $timestamp=timestamp$ Abnormal Security: Threat detected - $threat_type$ from $sender_email=email$ to $recipient_email=email$ Subject: $subject$

metadata_details.purpose: Parser for Abnormal Security logs using JSON parsing to normalise events into the AI-SIEM schema.
metadata_details.dependency_summary: Requires ingestion of Abnormal Security logs.
metadata_details.performance_impact: Minimal – this parser processes JSON formatted security logs.
metadata_details.author: Kenny Cho

semantic_summary: Parser processes Abnormal Security email security and authentication logs, focusing on email threats, user authentication events, and security findings. It normalizes events into standardized formats capturing email-based threats, authentication attempts, and security incidents with associated metadata.
optimization_opportunities: Add validation for email format consistency
Implement error handling for malformed timestamps

metadata_details.purpose: Parser for generic logs migrated from samples-main
metadata_details.dependency_summary: Requires ingestion of generic logs in appropriate format
metadata_details.author: Joel Mora

semantic_summary: Parser designed to process agent metrics logs from a time-series database (tsdb) source, capturing monitor metrics with timestamps, instance identifiers, and numerical values. Handles both quoted and non-quoted key-value pairs in log entries.
optimization_opportunities: Add data validation for metric values
Include error handling for malformed entries

formats.format: $timestamp$ AkamaiCDN streamId=$quoted_string{name=stream_id}$ cp=$quoted_string{name=config_id}$ reqId=$quoted_string{name=request_id}$ statusCode=$status_code{name=status_code}$ cliIP=$quoted_string{name=client_ip}$ reqHost=$quoted_string{name=host}$ reqMethod=$quoted_string{name=method}$ reqPath=$quoted_string{name=path}$ bytes=$field_value{name=bytes}$ cacheStatus=$quoted_string{name=cache_status}$ turnAroundTimeMSec=$field_value{name=response_time_ms}$ edgeIP=$quoted_string{name=edge_ip}$ country=$quoted_string{name=country}$ city=$quoted_string{name=city}$

metadata_details.purpose: OCSF-compliant parser for Akamai CDN access logs. Maps CDN request/response data to HTTP Activity class (4002) with proper field normalization for caching status, geographic location, and performance metrics.
metadata_details.sample_record:
2025-08-06T23:00:05Z AkamaiCDN streamId="stream-456" cp="12345" reqId="abcde12345" statusCode=200 cliIP="198.51.100.40" reqHost="www.example.com" reqMethod="GET" reqPath="/index.html" bytes=10240 cacheStatus="HIT" turnAroundTimeMSec=50 edgeIP="203.0.113.210" country="US" city="Seattle"
2025-08-06T23:01:20Z AkamaiCDN streamId="stream-456" cp="12345" reqId="fghij67890" statusCode=404 cliIP="203.0.113.90" reqHost="www.example.com" reqMethod="GET" reqPath="/unknown" bytes=512 cacheStatus="MISS" turnAroundTimeMSec=75 edgeIP="203.0.113.211" country="US" city="Chicago"
metadata_details.dependency_summary: Requires syslog ingestion from Akamai CDN with consistent key-value format including client IP, host, method, path, status code, cache status, and geographic information.
metadata_details.performance_impact: Minimal – single format parser with efficient field extraction and OCSF normalization.
metadata_details.tags: akamai, cdn, http, web, performance, ocsf, parser
metadata_details.author: Community

semantic_summary: This parser processes Akamai CDN access logs, extracting HTTP request/response data including client IPs, cache status, geographic location, and performance metrics. It normalizes these fields into OCSF-compliant format for network activity monitoring and web traffic analysis. The parser specifically handles CDN-specific attributes like cache hits/misses and edge server interactions.

factors: Multiple regex patterns for field extraction

optimization_opportunities: Implement error handling for malformed logs

formats.format: $timestamp$ AkamaiDNS streamId=$quoted_string{name=stream_id}$ cliIP=$quoted_string{name=client_ip}$ resolverIP=$quoted_string{name=resolver_ip}$ domain=$quoted_string{name=domain}$ recordType=$quoted_string{name=record_type}$ responseCode=$quoted_string{name=response_code}$ answer=$quoted_string{name=answer}$ edge=$quoted_string{name=edge_server}$ ttl=$field_value{name=ttl}$ bytes=$field_value{name=bytes}$

metadata_details.purpose: OCSF-compliant parser for Akamai DNS query logs. Maps DNS resolution requests and responses to DNS Activity class (4003) with proper normalization of query types, response codes, and geographic distribution data.
metadata_details.sample_record:
2025-08-06T22:30:01Z AkamaiDNS streamId="dns-123" cliIP="198.51.100.30" resolverIP="203.0.113.200" domain="www.example.com" recordType="A" responseCode="NOERROR" answer="192.0.2.5" edge="edge-ldn" ttl=300 bytes=128
2025-08-06T22:31:15Z AkamaiDNS streamId="dns-123" cliIP="203.0.113.60" resolverIP="203.0.113.200" domain="nonexistent.example.com" recordType="A" responseCode="NXDOMAIN" answer="" edge="edge-sfo" ttl=0 bytes=96
metadata_details.dependency_summary: Requires syslog ingestion from Akamai DNS infrastructure with consistent key-value format including client IP, resolver IP, domain name, record type, response code, and edge server information.
metadata_details.performance_impact: Minimal – single format parser with efficient DNS field extraction and OCSF normalization.
metadata_details.tags: akamai, dns, resolution, network, ocsf, parser
metadata_details.author: Community

semantic_summary: This parser processes Akamai DNS query logs, extracting and normalizing DNS resolution data including client IPs, domains queried, record types, and response codes. It maps these to OCSF DNS Activity class (4003) for standardized security monitoring of DNS infrastructure activity and potential DNS-based threats.

factors: Multiple regex patterns for field extraction

optimization_opportunities: Consolidate regex patterns for IP addresses
Add validation for TTL and bytes fields
Implement error handling for malformed records

formats.format: $timestamp$ AkamaiSecurity clientIP=$quoted_string{name=client_ip}$ host=$quoted_string{name=host}$ path=$quoted_string{name=path}$ ruleId=$quoted_string{name=rule_id}$ attackType=$quoted_string{name=attack_type}$ action=$quoted_string{name=action}$ httpMethod=$quoted_string{name=http_method}$ status=$field_value{name=status_code}$ userAgent=$quoted_string{name=user_agent}$ message=$quoted_string{name=message}$

metadata_details.purpose: OCSF-compliant parser for Akamai Security events. Maps web application security alerts and attacks to Security Control class (2001) with proper normalization of attack types, actions, and threat intelligence.
metadata_details.sample_record:
2025-08-06T12:30:55Z AkamaiSecurity clientIP="198.51.100.24" host="www.example.com" path="/login" ruleId="981176" attackType="SQL_Injection" action="blocked" httpMethod="POST" status=403 userAgent="Mozilla/5.0" message="Attempted SQL injection in login parameter detected and blocked"
2025-08-06T12:31:10Z AkamaiSecurity clientIP="198.51.100.24" host="www.example.com" path="/register" ruleId="941130" attackType="Cross_Site_Scripting" action="alert" httpMethod="POST" status=200 userAgent="Mozilla/5.0" message="Potential XSS pattern detected; request allowed with alert"
metadata_details.dependency_summary: Requires syslog ingestion from Akamai Security infrastructure with consistent key-value format including client IP, attack type, rule ID, action taken, and HTTP context.
metadata_details.performance_impact: Minimal – single format parser with efficient security event field extraction and OCSF normalization.
metadata_details.tags: akamai, security, waf, web_security, attacks, ocsf, parser
metadata_details.author: Community

semantic_summary: This parser processes Akamai Security WAF events, specifically handling web application security incidents including SQL injection, XSS, API scanning, and command injection attacks. It normalizes these security events into OCSF-compliant format, capturing attack details, client information, HTTP context, and security dispositions.

factors: Multiple regex patterns for field extraction

optimization_opportunities: Implement error handling for malformed messages
Consider batch processing for observables generation

metadata_details.purpose: Parser for Akamai SiteDefender WAF logs using the OCSF mapping to normalise events into the AI-SIEM schema.
metadata_details.dependency_summary: Requires ingestion of the original Akamai SiteDefender WAF logs.
metadata_details.performance_impact: Minimal – this parser processes JSON-formatted WAF events.
metadata_details.tags: akamai, sitedefender, waf, http, security, ocsf, parser

semantic_summary: Parser for Akamai SiteDefender WAF logs that processes security events including attack data, HTTP request details, and geolocation information. It normalizes WAF security findings into OCSF schema with focus on threat detection and response activities.

factors: Conditional transformations based on rule actions

optimization_opportunities: Implement error handling for malformed JSON

metadata_details.purpose: Parser for Apache Http logs using the OCSF mapping to normalise events into the AI-SIEM schema.
metadata_details.dependency_summary: Requires ingestion of the original Apache Http logs.
metadata_details.performance_impact: Minimal – this parser is a placeholder pointing to the upstream definition.

semantic_summary: Apache HTTP server log parser that processes web access logs, extracting key HTTP request/response data including client IP, user agent, timestamps, request methods, URLs, and response codes. Maps to OCSF schema for standardized security event representation with focus on web activity monitoring.

factors: Multiple format patterns to handle log variations

optimization_opportunities: Add support for additional HTTP methods
Include error handling for malformed logs
Add parsing for additional HTTP headers
Implement caching for repeated pattern matching

semantic_summary: Parser configuration for Aruba ClearPass security logs handling multiple log types including authentication sessions, system events, audit records, and RADIUS logs. Implements standardized timestamp parsing and IPv4 extraction with OCSF field mapping for security event normalization.
optimization_opportunities: Consolidate similar format patterns using shared regex components
Add field validation for IP addresses
Implement more specific field parsing for details section
Add error handling for malformed timestamps

metadata_details.author: Joel Mora

semantic_summary: Parser designed to process AWS CloudWatch logs, specifically handling both JSON formatted logs and space-delimited VPC flow logs. Primary purpose is to normalize AWS infrastructure logging for security monitoring and analysis.

factors: Basic JSON parsing with dottedJson option

optimization_opportunities: Add field validation for VPC flow log format
Add explicit error handling for malformed JSON
Consider adding support for additional CloudWatch log formats
